questions.q: Marthanda Varma is best remembered as the founder and consolidator of which south Indian kingdom?
questions.explanation: Marthanda Varma built Travancore into a strong, centralised state in the 18th century CE.

questions.q: In which century did Marthanda Varma rule and consolidate his kingdom?
questions.explanation: Marthanda Varma reigned in the 18th century CE, ascending the throne in 1729.

questions.q: Travancore, the kingdom Marthanda Varma built, lay in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Travancore occupied the southern part of present-day Kerala, with adjoining areas of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Which European trading company did Marthanda Varma defeat at the Battle of Colachel?
questions.explanation: He defeated the Dutch East India Company (VOC) at Colachel in 1741.

questions.q: In which year did Marthanda Varma win the Battle of Colachel?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Colachel was fought and won in 1741 CE.

questions.q: Which deity did Marthanda Varma dedicate his kingdom to in a famous act of surrender of sovereignty?
questions.explanation: He dedicated Travancore to Sri Padmanabhaswamy (a form of Vishnu) in the act known as Tripadidanam.

questions.q: Marthanda Varma belonged to which royal house of Travancore?
questions.explanation: He belonged to the Travancore royal house, which had grown out of the older Venad kingdom.

questions.q: After dedicating the kingdom to the deity, the rulers of Travancore governed under which humble title?
questions.explanation: The kings ruled as Padmanabha Dasa, meaning servant of Lord Padmanabha.

questions.q: What kind of state did Marthanda Varma create out of Travancore?
questions.explanation: He is credited with forging a strong, centralised state from fragmented territories.

questions.q: The Battle of Colachel was fought along which geographic feature of Travancore?
options: A river delta in the north
questions.explanation: Colachel was a coastal engagement against the Dutch, a sea-trading power.

questions.q: In which year did Marthanda Varma ascend the throne of Travancore?
questions.explanation: Marthanda Varma became ruler of Travancore in 1729 CE.

questions.q: The Battle of Colachel is often noted as one of the earliest examples of what in Asian history?
options: Use of war elephants by Europeans
The first use of cannon in India
An Asian power defeating a European naval power
questions.explanation: Colachel is frequently cited as an early instance of an Asian state defeating a European colonial naval power.

questions.q: Which captured Dutch naval commander later took service with and helped modernise the Travancore army?
questions.explanation: Eustachius De Lannoy was captured at Colachel and became a trusted commander who modernised Travancore's forces.

questions.q: Marthanda Varma suppressed a powerful group of feudal lords who had long curbed royal authority. Who were they?
questions.explanation: He crushed the Ettuveettil Pillamar, the eight noble houses that had constrained earlier rulers.

questions.q: Which prosperous spice trade did Marthanda Varma bring under a royal monopoly to fund his state?
questions.explanation: He established a royal monopoly over the lucrative pepper and spice trade of the Malabar-Travancore coast.

questions.q: Which neighbouring kingdom did Marthanda Varma decisively expand against, absorbing territories northward?
options: The Kingdom of Cochin and Kayamkulam
questions.explanation: He annexed Kayamkulam and other northern principalities, expanding Travancore toward Cochin's borders.

questions.q: The defensive barrier built during Travancore's expansion to guard its northern frontier is known as what?
questions.explanation: The Travancore Lines, also called the Nedumkotta, were fortifications guarding the kingdom's northern approaches.

questions.q: Which famous temple did Marthanda Varma extensively rebuild and enrich at his capital?
questions.explanation: He rebuilt and grandly endowed the Sri Padmanabhaswamy Temple at Thiruvananthapuram.

questions.q: What was the principal capital of Travancore associated with Marthanda Varma's reign?
questions.explanation: Thiruvananthapuram became the heart of Travancore, centred on the Padmanabhaswamy temple.

questions.q: The ceremonial dedication of the kingdom to Sri Padmanabha is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Tripadidanam was Marthanda Varma's surrender of the realm to the deity, ruling thereafter as his servant.

questions.q: Which long-feared Indian power did Travancore, fortified by Marthanda Varma's successors, later have to resist?
questions.explanation: The Nedumkotta lines later helped Travancore resist Mysore's forces under Tipu Sultan.

questions.q: On which date is the Battle of Colachel traditionally dated?
questions.explanation: The decisive defeat of the Dutch at Colachel is generally dated to 10 August 1741.

questions.q: Eustachius De Lannoy, the Dutch officer who served Travancore, came to be popularly known by what title?
questions.explanation: De Lannoy was honoured as the Valiya Kappitan, or Great Captain, for his service to Travancore.

questions.q: Travancore's royal succession, as in much of Kerala, followed which system of inheritance?
questions.explanation: Travancore followed Marumakkathayam, a matrilineal system in which succession passed through the female line.

questions.q: Marthanda Varma's reign ended with his death in which year?
questions.explanation: Marthanda Varma died in 1758 CE, succeeded by his nephew Dharma Raja.

questions.q: Who succeeded Marthanda Varma and continued his policies, earning the epithet Dharma Raja?
questions.explanation: His nephew Rama Varma, known as Karthika Thirunal Dharma Raja, succeeded and consolidated his legacy.

questions.q: The older kingdom from which Marthanda Varma's Travancore developed was historically called what?
questions.explanation: Travancore evolved from the earlier kingdom of Venad in the far south of Kerala.

questions.q: Marthanda Varma's deportation of rebellious feudal nobles is recorded alongside his crushing of which conspiracy linked to a rival claimant?
questions.explanation: He suppressed the revolt of the Thampi brothers, rival claimants backed by the Ettuveettil Pillamar.

questions.q: Which novel by C. V. Raman Pillai, a landmark of Malayalam literature, is set against the rise of Marthanda Varma?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman Pillai's historical novel Marthandavarma (1891) dramatises the king's struggle for the throne.

questions.q: By dedicating Travancore to Sri Padmanabha, Marthanda Varma effectively made the deity what in relation to the state?
options: The titular sovereign, with the king as servant-regent
questions.explanation: The deity became the titular sovereign of Travancore, while the king ruled as Padmanabha Dasa, his servant.
